_comment: PULSE 2.0 — Externalized Data Configuration. Edit this file to load your own organisation's data into the mockup. See PULSE_data_guide.md for field definitions and instructions.

roles.title: Senior Full Stack Developer
drivers: AI code generation at 70%+ acceptance rate in production
PR review and code analysis AI-automated
Architecture decisions remain human-led

roles.title: Junior Full Stack Developer
drivers: Feature implementation tasks fully AI-generatable
Entry-level work absorbed into senior+AI pairing model
No differentiated skill resisting automation

roles.title: Product Owner
drivers: Stakeholder management requires human relationship skills
Vision and strategy not automatable
Backlog prioritisation AI-assisted but judgment-dependent

roles.title: UX Designer
drivers: UI generation tools maturing rapidly
Component library creation largely automatable
User research and facilitation remain human-led

roles.title: Senior Full Stack Developer
drivers: Same profile as Mobile Pod counterpart
Complex business logic still needs architecture oversight
AI pair programming at production quality

roles.title: QA Engineer
drivers: Test script generation fully automated
Regression suite AI-managed end-to-end
Exploratory and edge-case testing remains human

roles.title: API Integration Specialist
drivers: Integration boilerplate code 80%+ generatable
API documentation and schema auto-generated
Domain expertise in financial protocols still required

roles.title: DevOps Engineer
drivers: IaC and pipeline configuration AI-generated at high quality
Runbook documentation fully automated
Incident reasoning and platform governance remain human

roles.title: SAP Functional Consultant
drivers: Configuration docs and functional specs AI-assisted
Process design requires business domain knowledge
Regulatory mapping needs expert human sign-off

roles.title: ABAP Developer
drivers: ABAP code generation reaching production quality
Enhancement development largely automatable
Core customisation logic still needs SAP expertise

roles.title: Core Banking Integration Dev
drivers: Integration pattern code 70% AI-generatable
Legacy system mapping AI-assisted
Regulatory mapping requires human accountability

roles.title: Business Analyst
drivers: Requirements documents AI-generated from interview notes
Process maps and gap analyses automated
Stakeholder facilitation and negotiation remain human

roles.title: Payments Engineer
drivers: Payment scheme logic requires regulatory expertise
Integration code AI-assisted but scheme knowledge critical
Real-time system design needs senior oversight

roles.title: Test Analyst — Payments
drivers: Payment test scripts fully AI-generatable
End-to-end regression fully automated
UAT coordination still requires human stakeholder management

drivers: ETL pipeline code generation highly automatable
Data quality checks AI-driven
Risk model implementation requires domain expertise

roles.title: Risk Reporting Analyst
drivers: SQL and report generation fully automatable
Variance commentary AI-generated at publication quality
Regulatory submission sign-off requires human accountability

roles.title: GRC Analyst
drivers: Policy drafting AI-assisted but judgment-intensive
Control testing AI-augmented significantly
Regulatory interpretation requires certified expertise

roles.title: Cybersecurity Analyst
drivers: L1 alert triage AI-automated at 80%
Incident report drafting AI-assisted
Threat hunting and novel attack analysis human-led

roles.title: Penetration Tester
drivers: Report writing AI-assisted — significant time saving
Standard scan execution automated
Novel exploit identification requires creative human reasoning

roles.title: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
drivers: Terraform / IaC AI-generated at high quality
Cloud cost optimisation AI-managed
Architecture and platform decisions need senior oversight

roles.title: Network Engineer
drivers: Physical configuration requires hands-on access
Network runbooks AI-generated — time saving
Complex fault diagnosis remains human-led

drivers: 80% of L1 tickets resolvable by AI agents today
Password resets and access provisioning fully automated
Complex escalations require human judgement and empathy

roles.title: IT Service Manager
drivers: Vendor relationship management requires human negotiation
SLA governance needs strategic context
Reporting and dashboards AI-automated — time saving

horizon12.reasoning: Within 12 months this role retains full employment but shifts fundamentally toward AI orchestration — steering and validating AI-generated code rather than authoring it. Senior developers who invest in AI tooling now will see productivity multipliers of 3–5×, making them disproportionately valuable. Those who delay will find the market has moved without them.

horizon24.reasoning: By 24 months this role converges with Solution Architect in AI-mature organisations. A single senior engineer orchestrating AI agents across the full stack replaces the traditional Senior/Junior pairing. Recommend redesigning the role profile now to attract the next generation of AI-native engineers.

horizon12.reasoning: The 40% obsolescence probability at 12 months is the strongest near-term signal in this analysis. Junior developers perform tasks now at the frontier of production AI capability. Organisations should cease new junior developer hiring immediately and redirect learning investment to reskilling existing staff toward AI-augmented delivery roles.

horizon24.reasoning: The standalone junior developer role is unlikely to exist in its current form in high-AI-adoption organisations by 2028. HR should begin proactive redeployment planning, particularly for offshore junior headcount which faces the sharpest reduction.

horizon12.reasoning: L1 support is the most immediately automatable function in the IT division. AI agents handle 60–80% of L1 ticket types at production quality today. The 12 offshore IT Support Specialists face direct displacement within 12–18 months. Immediate hiring freeze recommended.

horizon24.reasoning: The Service Desk in its current form will not exist by 2028. The new structure consists of a small onsite team managing AI agents, handling complex issues, and owning the end-user experience strategy. Begin structured workforce transition planning for this cohort now.

horizon12.reasoning: Risk report generation is automatable at publication quality today. One analyst with AI tools replaces three without. FINMA and ECB regulatory frameworks are beginning to require documented AI governance for automated reporting, creating a new specialisation.

horizon24.reasoning: The Risk AI Analyst combines data literacy, regulatory expertise, and AI output governance. Current headcount of 6 reduces to 2–3 under the Likely scenario. Plan redeployment of the rest proactively, not reactively.

horizon12.reasoning: DevOps engineers who adopt AI tooling become dramatically more productive. The transformation reflects a role identity shift from 'builds pipelines' to 'governs the platform AI agents run on.' Early movers gain significant strategic value in a market where AI deployment expertise is scarce.

horizon24.reasoning: The platform engineering role of 2028 is fundamentally about making AI agents reliable, observable, and governable. This role transformation should be treated as an opportunity — one of the few in this analysis where the outcome is upskilling rather than displacement.

horizon12.reasoning: Penetration testing is among the most resilient roles in this analysis. The adversarial, creative nature of offensive security requires human intuition that AI cannot replicate. Augmentation is real — testers using AI tools are 40–60% more productive — but displacement risk is low.

horizon24.reasoning: By 24 months the role evolves toward AI Red Teaming — testing AI systems themselves for vulnerabilities. This is a growth area mandated by emerging AI governance regulation. Organisations should invest in upskilling this cohort toward AI security specialisation.

conservative.assumption: AI code generation at ~35% acceptance. Junior roles reduce through attrition only.
conservative.reasoning: Conservative scenario assumes cautious adoption. Headcount reduces through natural attrition. Junior developer roles partially sustained. Cost saving of 8% reflects productivity gain without structural change.
conservative.risk: If AI adoption accelerates beyond assumption, savings are left on the table and competitors gain structural cost advantage.
conservative.workforceImpact: Offshore impact minimal. Junior developer attrition not backfilled. No active redeployment required.
leadershipActions: Monitor AI acceptance rates quarterly
Begin voluntary reskilling for 2 junior developers
Review junior hiring policy

likely.assumption: AI code generation reaches 65% acceptance within 18 months. Senior+AI pairing model deployed.
likely.reasoning: The Likely scenario reflects the Senior+AI pairing model: one senior developer with AI tools delivers the output of 2–3 without. Offshore junior headcount reduces from 8 to 3. A new AI Prompt Engineering role emerges. Pod shrinks from 17 to 10 FTEs — same delivery capacity, 31% lower cost base.
likely.risk: AI adoption may be delayed by compliance review cycles or model reliability concerns in production banking environments.
likely.workforceImpact: 6 offshore junior developers impacted. 2 reskilling candidates identified. 3 require managed transition. 1 nearshore UX contractor role reduced.
leadershipActions: Structured reskilling for 2 junior devs → AI Prompt Engineer pathway
Managed transition plan for 3 offshore roles
Redesign UX Designer role profile
Hire or reskill 1 AI Prompt Engineer

aggressive.assumption: Full agentic coding deployment. AI generates, tests, and deploys features with minimal human authoring.
aggressive.reasoning: Three senior engineers orchestrate AI agents for the full delivery pipeline. All 8 offshore junior roles eliminated. Pod of 6 replaces Pod of 17 — 54% cost reduction.
aggressive.risk: High. Banking regulatory requirements for human oversight may constrain autonomous deployment. Significant transition risk if timeline is compressed.
aggressive.workforceImpact: 8 offshore junior roles eliminated. Active redeployment to other streams or exit required. Full offshore presence in this Pod eliminated.
leadershipActions: Board-level approval required
Regulatory counsel on autonomous code deployment
Structured 18-month exit programme for offshore cohort
Executive sponsorship of Agentic deployment programme

conservative.assumption: AI chatbot resolves 40% of L1 tickets. Human agents handle remaining volume.
conservative.reasoning: Maintains bulk of offshore service desk while AI handles routine ticket types. Headcount reduces from 12 to 8 through attrition.
conservative.risk: Conservative assumption may prove optimistic if AI support agents reach the Likely scenario capability earlier than expected.
conservative.workforceImpact: 4 offshore roles eliminated through attrition over 18–24 months. No active transition required.
leadershipActions: Deploy AI chatbot for top 5 ticket types
Measure resolution rate quarterly
Do not backfill natural attrition in offshore cohort

likely.assumption: AI agents resolve 75% of L1 and L2 tickets autonomously. Humans handle exceptions and sensitive issues.
likely.reasoning: 12 offshore IT Support roles reduce to 2 complex-case specialists. Three new roles emerge. Cost saving of CHF 1.4M annually for this Pod alone. Offshore headcount from 12 to 2.
likely.risk: Transition complexity is high. Poor AI agent experiences during transition can cause significant user trust damage.
likely.workforceImpact: 10 offshore IT Support roles impacted. 2 highest-performers reskilled to Complex Support Specialist (onsite). Remaining 10 managed transition over 12 months.
leadershipActions: Define Complex Support Specialist job profile immediately
Select 2 offshore high-performers for onsite reskilling
Design AI Agent Supervisor training programme
Commission AI service desk platform evaluation
Communicate transparently with offshore team leads

aggressive.assumption: End-to-end agentic service desk. AI handles all routine and 90% of complex tickets.
aggressive.reasoning: Fully automated service desk with a small onsite team of three. All offshore presence eliminated. Technically achievable within 36 months but requires significant change management.
aggressive.risk: Critical. Full elimination of offshore presence in 36 months requires immediate planning. Staff morale and reputational risk of large-scale redundancies.
aggressive.workforceImpact: All 12 offshore IT Support roles eliminated. Full managed transition programme required with 18-month lead time minimum.
leadershipActions: Legal review of offshore employment obligations in India and Philippines
Board risk register — reputational and operational
Define 24-month managed transition with severance framework
Engage CHRO and Legal before any communication to offshore teams

conservative.assumption: AI assists report generation. All outputs require mandatory human review per regulatory requirement.
conservative.reasoning: Maintains data engineering intact and reduces analyst headcount modestly through attrition. FINMA regulatory caution constrains full automation.
conservative.risk: Regulatory interpretation may be more permissive than assumed, making this scenario overly cautious.
conservative.workforceImpact: 2 offshore analyst roles eliminated through attrition. No active transition required.
leadershipActions: Engage Risk Legal on regulatory AI governance framework
Deploy AI reporting tools to reduce analyst workload
Assess which reports qualify for AI-assisted generation

likely.assumption: AI generates 80% of standard risk reports. Analysts focus on exception analysis and regulatory narrative.
likely.reasoning: The Risk AI Analyst combines data literacy, regulatory expertise, and AI output governance. 4 offshore analysts reduced to 2 specialist roles. Location moves from offshore Bangalore to nearshore Warsaw for surviving roles.
likely.risk: Risk AI Analyst profile is scarce in the market. Reskilling from existing cohort may take 18 months.
likely.workforceImpact: 4 offshore analysts impacted. 2 identified for reskilling to Risk AI Analyst (nearshore). Remaining 2 managed transition with 12-month lead time.
leadershipActions: Define Risk AI Analyst job profile and reskilling pathway
Engage FINMA AI governance consultation
Identify 2 highest-capability offshore analysts for nearshore reskilling
Commission AI reporting platform assessment

aggressive.assumption: Near-autonomous risk reporting with continuous AI monitoring and exception alerting.
aggressive.reasoning: Offshore presence eliminated as remaining roles require regulatory judgment and business proximity. A new Regulatory AI Officer manages the interface with FINMA/ECB.
aggressive.risk: Regulatory AI Officer is a net-new role with no established market salary. FINMA guidance on automated risk reporting is still developing.
aggressive.workforceImpact: 6 offshore analyst roles impacted. 2 reskilled to Risk AI Analyst. Remaining 4 managed exit over 18 months.
leadershipActions: Immediate engagement with FINMA on AI governance obligations
External search for Regulatory AI Officer — 6 month lead time
Structured 18-month transition for 4 offshore roles

executiveSummary.headline: 52% of the IT workforce operates in roles with high AI displacement exposure — concentrated in functions that are already being actively automated.
executiveSummary.body: Analysis of 35 roles across 357 FTE reveals a division at a structural inflection point. The near-term risk is not mass redundancy — it is a quiet hollowing out of junior and routine roles, particularly in offshore locations, while senior roles transform into AI orchestration functions. Without deliberate action, the organisation risks both talent pipeline disruption and missed productivity gains.

findings.title: Junior and routine roles face imminent displacement — particularly offshore
findings.body: Junior developers, test analysts, and IT support specialists — the majority offshore — are performing tasks that AI tools can now execute at production quality. The Likely scenario projects a 51% reduction in offshore headcount (148 → 72 FTE) within 24 months. Hiring of junior profiles into exposed roles should be reviewed immediately.

findings.title: Senior roles are transforming, not disappearing — but the window to act is narrow
findings.body: Senior engineers, DevOps professionals, and data engineers face high theoretical exposure but low near-term obsolescence risk. Their roles are transforming into AI orchestration, platform governance, and system design functions. Organisations that reskill this cohort now will see productivity gains of 40–60%.

findings.title: Five new AI-native roles are emerging that do not currently exist in the division
findings.body: AI Orchestrator, AI Platform Engineer, Risk AI Analyst, AI Agent Supervisor, and Regulatory AI Officer are roles the division will need within 18–24 months. None exist today. Building these capabilities through reskilling is faster and cheaper than external hiring — but requires structured programmes starting now.

actions.title: Freeze junior hiring in high-exposure roles
actions.body: Halt new recruitment for Junior Developer, Test Analyst, and L1 Support roles in offshore locations. Redirect headcount budget to AI reskilling programmes for existing senior staff.

actions.title: Launch AI reskilling programme for top 40 exposed roles
actions.body: Design structured reskilling pathways for Senior Developers, DevOps Engineers, and Business Analysts. Target: 60% of high-exposure FTE enrolled within 9 months.

actions.title: Redesign Pod structure for AI-augmented delivery
actions.body: Begin Pod redesign based on Scenario Explorer projections. Pilot with Mobile Pod and Service Desk Pod. Establish new AI-native roles. Revise offshore strategy in line with Likely scenario projections.
